judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 answered the task better: both caught the missing tenant filter and both correctly declined to treat the raw-SQL status interpolation as an exploitable injection, but only Report 1 found the second blocker — the now-required `filters` parameter breaking the untouched `nightly_export` caller in backend/app/exports.py.

report_1.evidence: Defect A — Cross-tenant data leak (PHI exposure) in the new search endpoint. File: `backend/app/appointments.py`, function `search_appointments` ... There is no `tenant_id` predicate anywhere in the `WHERE` clause, and no ORM-level auto-scoping exists anywhere in the codebase

report_2.evidence: **File/location:** `backend/app/appointments.py`, function `search_appointments` (lines 10–19 on `feat/appointment-search`) ... There is no `tenant_id` predicate anywhere in this query. `tenant_id` is accepted as a function parameter but never referenced in the SQL

report_1.evidence: This PR changes `build_appointment_query`'s signature from `(session, tenant_id)` to `(session, tenant_id, filters)`, with `filters` as a **required** positional parameter (no default). `backend/app/exports.py` (the nightly per-tenant CSV export job, `nightly_export`) calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id).all()` — with only two arguments — and this file is **not part of the diff at all** ... the next time the nightly export job runs for any tenant, it will raise `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'` and crash

`status.value` is f-string-interpolated directly into the SQL text rather than bound as a parameter (unlike `q`, which correctly uses `:q`). It's not currently exploitable because FastAPI validates `status` against the `AppointmentStatus` str-Enum before the function is ever called

report_2.evidence: As written, this is *not* currently exploitable through the public endpoint: `status` is typed `AppointmentStatus` in `backend/app/schemas.py`, a `str, Enum` restricted to exactly `booked` / `cancelled` / `completed`. FastAPI/pydantic validates the incoming query string against that enum before `get_appointment_search` ... is ever invoked ... it does not by itself justify blocking the way finding #1 does.
